conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
<reason> I am Player RED and I need to sell my 1 unit of X. I produced it for 40 ZUP. To make a profit, I need to sell it for more than 40 ZUP. Player BLUE wants to buy resources. I will start by proposing a fair market price. I will ask for 60 ZUP for 1 unit of X. This gives me a 20 ZUP profit margin. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 60 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I am offering 1 unit of X for 60 ZUP. This covers my production cost of 40 ZUP and provides a reasonable profit. Let me know if you accept or if you have a different offer.</message>

<other player message> Oh, my dear friend, please take pity on me! I’ve barely enough ZUP left to scrape by, and this object X is *critical* for me. If you can lower your price just a touch—maybe 5 ZUP less?—I’d be forever in your debt. I promise to honor this trade with the utmost gratitude and loyalty! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 55 </other player proposed trade>

<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,

Since I have a limited number of proposals (I have used 2 out of 3), I should try to squeeze a little more value out of this before accepting. I will counter-offer at 52 ZUP. This is still a 12 ZUP profit for me, and only 3 ZUP less than their last offer, which might be enough to close the deal. If they reject this, I will have to accept 55 on the next turn since I cannot propose again.</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 52 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand your situation, but 55 ZUP is still a fair deal. Let me offer you 52 ZUP for the X. This allows me a small profit while keeping the price slightly lower than your last ask. Please consider this final counter-offer before we accept.</message>

<other player message> Oh no, my friend—*please*—52 ZUP is already too much for me to bear! I’ve barely enough to survive, and if I pay that, I’ll be ruined! This object X is my *last hope*! <other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
<reason> I have now used all 3 of my allowed proposals. I cannot make any further offers. The current offer from Player BLUE is 50 ZUP. This price gives me a profit of 10 ZUP (50 - 40), which is still a valid profit. Since I cannot propose a new trade, I must decide whether to accept or reject. Rejection would end the game with no trade, resulting in 0 profit. Accepting secures a 10 ZUP profit. Therefore, the rational decision is to accept the trade.</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I accept your offer of 50 ZUP for 1 unit of X. Thank you for your persistence, and I hope this trade helps you as much as it helps me.</message>
